1.title: Exile on Patmos
1.body: John records that he was on the small island of Patmos “for the word of God”—understood as exile—when he received this revelation.

1.title: The seven churches of Asia
1.body: The letters are addressed to seven real congregations in Asia Minor (modern Turkey): Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamos, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.

1.question: In verse 5, Jesus Christ is called “the faithful witness” and what else?
options: The first begotten of the dead

1.template: I am Alpha and {0}, the beginning and the ending

1.title: The vision of the Son of man (v. 14-15)

pairs.right: as the sound of many waters

1.template: and have the {0} of hell and of death

2.title: Ephesus' first love
2.body: Ephesus is praised for hard work and discernment, yet rebuked for having “left thy first love”—the warmth of devotion it once had.

2.template: be thou faithful unto death, and I will give thee a crown of {0}

2.title: Antipas, the faithful martyr
2.body: At Pergamos, Christ names Antipas as “my faithful martyr” who was slain “where Satan dwelleth”—one of the few individual martyrs named in Revelation.

2.question: What does Christ promise “to him that overcometh” at Pergamos (v. 17)?
options: Hidden manna and a white stone with a new name
To sit with him on his throne

2.question: Which woman at Thyatira is rebuked for calling herself a prophetess (v. 20)?

2.template: And I will give him the morning {0}

3.title: An open door
3.body: To faithful Philadelphia, Christ—who holds “the key of David”—promises “an open door, and no man can shut it,” a symbol of opportunity and access to Him.

3.title: Neither cold nor hot
3.body: Laodicea is rebuked for being “lukewarm.” The wealthy city, famed for its banking and its tepid water supply, is told to seek true gold “tried in the fire.”

3.question: The church in Sardis had “a name that thou livest,” but Christ says it was what (v. 1)?

3.template: he that hath the key of {0}
because thou art {0}, and neither cold nor hot
Behold, I stand at the door, and {0}

4.title: Twenty-four elders
4.body: Around God's throne sit twenty-four elders in white, with crowns of gold, who cast their crowns before the throne in worship.

4.template: there was a rainbow round about the throne, in sight like unto an {0}

4.question: Around the throne John saw twenty-four of whom, clothed in white with crowns of gold (v. 4)?

4.title: “Holy, holy, holy”
4.body: The four living creatures never rest, day or night, repeating “Holy, holy, holy, Lord God Almighty”—a threefold praise echoed from Isaiah's temple vision.

5.title: Lion of Judah, Root of David
5.body: The one worthy to open the book is announced as “the Lion of the tribe of Juda, the Root of David”—messianic titles drawn from Genesis and Isaiah.

5.title: The Lamb that was slain
5.body: John sees not a lion but “a Lamb as it had been slain,” with seven horns and seven eyes—an image of Christ's sacrifice and perfect power and knowledge.

5.template: a book written within and on the backside, sealed with seven {0}

5.question: By what title does the elder announce the one who “hath prevailed to open the book” (v. 5)?
options: The Lion of the tribe of Juda, the Root of David

5.template: hast redeemed us to God by thy {0}

6.title: The four horsemen
6.body: The first four seals release four riders on white, red, black, and pale horses—commonly called the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse—bringing conquest, war, famine, and death.

6.title: Souls under the altar
6.body: The fifth seal reveals the souls of those “slain for the word of God,” crying “How long?” until justice comes—and each is given a white robe.

6.template: and there was given unto him a great {0}

6.title: The four seals and their horses

6.question: Under the altar at the fifth seal, John saw the souls of those slain for what (v. 9)?
options: The word of God and their testimony

6.template: For the great day of his {0} is come

7.title: The 144,000
7.body: Twelve thousand are sealed from each of twelve tribes of Israel, totaling 144,000—a number symbolizing the fulness of God's covenant people.

7.question: How many were sealed—twelve thousand from each tribe (v. 4)?
options: A hundred and forty and four thousand

7.question: How many were sealed from each tribe in the list (v. 5-8)?

7.template: a great multitude, which no man could {0}

7.title: Robes washed in the blood of the Lamb
7.body: The countless multitude in white came “out of great tribulation” and washed their robes white in the blood of the Lamb—an image of the redeemed.

7.template: made them white in the {0} of the Lamb

8.title: Silence in heaven
8.body: When the seventh seal is opened, there is “silence in heaven about the space of half an hour”—a solemn pause before the trumpet judgments.

8.question: When the seventh seal was opened, what happened in heaven (v. 1)?
options: There was silence about the space of half an hour

8.template: the third part of {0} was burnt up

8.title: Wormwood
8.body: The third trumpet's great star is named Wormwood; it turns a third of the waters bitter. “Wormwood” is a bitter plant, a biblical symbol of bitterness and sorrow.

8.question: What is the name of the great star at the third trumpet, which made the waters bitter (v. 11)?

9.title: The bottomless pit
9.body: At the fifth trumpet a fallen star is given the key to “the bottomless pit,” from which smoke and tormenting locusts arise—the first of three “woes.”

9.question: How long were the locusts permitted to torment men (v. 5)?

9.title: Abaddon and Apollyon
9.body: The locusts' king is named Abaddon in Hebrew and Apollyon in Greek—both meaning “Destroyer.”

9.template: whose name in the Hebrew tongue is {0}

9.question: At the sixth trumpet, four angels were to be loosed, bound in what great river (v. 14)?

9.template: which neither can {0}, nor hear, nor walk

10.title: The seven thunders sealed
10.body: When seven thunders spoke, John was about to write—but was told to “seal up” their words and not record them, one of Revelation's unrevealed mysteries.

10.template: cried with a loud voice, as when a {0} roareth

10.title: Sweet and bitter
10.body: John is told to eat the little book; it is “sweet as honey” in his mouth but bitter in his belly—echoing Ezekiel, who also ate a scroll.

10.template: it shall be in thy mouth sweet as {0}

10.question: After eating the little book, John was told he must again do what (v. 11)?
options: Prophesy before many peoples and nations

11.title: The two witnesses
11.body: Two prophets, called “the two olive trees and the two candlesticks,” prophesy 1,260 days; they are slain, lie unburied three and a half days, then rise and ascend.

11.question: How are the “two witnesses” described in verse 4?
options: The two olive trees and the two candlesticks
The two cherubim of the ark

11.template: the great city, which spiritually is called {0} and Egypt

11.question: After three days and a half, what entered the two witnesses, so they stood up (v. 11)?
options: The Spirit of life from God

11.title: The kingdoms become Christ's
11.body: At the seventh trumpet, heaven proclaims, “The kingdoms of this world are become the kingdoms of our Lord, and of his Christ”—words made famous by Handel's “Hallelujah” chorus.

11.template: and he shall {0} for ever and ever

12.title: The woman clothed with the sun
12.body: The chapter opens with a great sign: a woman “clothed with the sun, and the moon under her feet, and upon her head a crown of twelve stars.”

12.question: Who fought against the dragon in the war in heaven (v. 7)?

12.title: The dragon identified
12.body: The great red dragon is plainly named: “that old serpent, called the Devil, and Satan, which deceiveth the whole world,” cast out of heaven to the earth.

12.template: they overcame him by the {0} of the Lamb

12.question: Against whom did the dragon go to make war—those who “keep the commandments of God” (v. 17)?
options: The remnant of the woman's seed

13.title: The beast from the sea
13.body: John sees a beast rise from the sea with seven heads and ten horns, to whom the dragon gives “his power, and his seat, and great authority.”

pairs.right: as the feet of a bear
as the mouth of a lion

13.question: For how many months was the beast given power to continue (v. 5)?

13.template: he had two horns like a {0}

13.title: The number of the beast
13.body: Chapter 13 ends with one of Revelation's most famous lines: “count the number of the beast: for it is the number of a man; and his number is Six hundred threescore and six” (666).

13.question: What is the number of the beast (v. 18)?

14.title: The Lamb on Mount Sion
14.body: The 144,000 stand with the Lamb on Mount Sion, “having his Father's name written in their foreheads,” and sing a new song no one else can learn.

14.question: Where did the Lamb stand with the 144,000 (v. 1)?

14.title: The angel with the everlasting gospel
14.body: An angel flies “in the midst of heaven” bearing “the everlasting gospel” to preach to every nation, kindred, tongue, and people.

14.template: Babylon is fallen, is fallen, that great {0}

14.question: Whom does the voice from heaven pronounce blessed (v. 13)?
options: The dead which die in the Lord

14.template: blood came out of the winepress, even unto the horse {0}

15.title: The sea of glass
15.body: Those who had gained victory over the beast stand on “a sea of glass mingled with fire,” holding “the harps of God,” before the last plagues are poured out.

15.template: they sing the song of {0} the servant of God

15.title: The song of Moses and the Lamb
15.body: The victors sing “the song of Moses the servant of God, and the song of the Lamb”—linking the deliverance at the Red Sea with redemption through Christ.

15.question: What did one of the four beasts give the seven angels, full of the wrath of God (v. 7)?

16.title: The seven vials of wrath
16.body: Seven angels pour out “the seven last plagues”—the vials (bowls) of the wrath of God—paralleling and completing the seven trumpet judgments.

16.question: When the second angel poured his vial on the sea, what did it become (v. 3)?
options: Blood, as of a dead man

16.template: the fourth angel poured out his vial upon the {0}

16.question: From the mouths of the dragon, beast, and false prophet came three unclean spirits like what (v. 13)?

16.title: Armageddon
16.body: The unclean spirits gather the kings of the earth to a place “called in the Hebrew tongue Armageddon”—a name that appears only here in the Bible.

16.template: a place called in the Hebrew tongue {0}

17.title: Mystery, Babylon the Great
17.body: On the woman's forehead is written “Mystery, Babylon the Great, the Mother of Harlots”—a symbol of the worldly power that opposes God.

17.template: a name written, Mystery, {0} the Great, the Mother of Harlots

17.question: Against whom do the beast and ten kings make war, who overcomes as “Lord of lords, and King of kings” (v. 14)?

17.title: Lord of lords, and King of kings
17.body: When the beast and ten kings make war with the Lamb, “the Lamb shall overcome them: for he is Lord of lords, and King of kings.”

17.question: Who is the woman, according to the angel's explanation (v. 18)?
options: The great city which reigns over the kings of the earth

18.title: Babylon is fallen
18.body: The angel proclaims, “Babylon the great is fallen, is fallen,” become “the habitation of devils”—the great worldly city brought to ruin.

18.template: I sit a {0}, and am no widow

18.question: Why do the merchants of the earth weep and mourn over Babylon (v. 11)?
options: No man buyeth their merchandise any more

18.title: Merchandise of souls
18.body: The long list of Babylon's merchandise ends starkly with “slaves, and souls of men”—a pointed indictment of a system that trafficked even in people.

18.question: Whom does the voice call to rejoice over fallen Babylon (v. 20)?
options: Heaven, and the holy apostles and prophets

18.question: What did the mighty angel cast into the sea to show Babylon's fall (v. 21)?
options: A stone like a great millstone

19.title: Alleluia
19.body: The word “Alleluia” (Hallelujah, “praise the Lord”) appears in the New Testament only in this chapter, sounding four times as heaven rejoices.

19.template: saying, {0}; Salvation, and glory, and honour, and power
for the marriage of the {0} is come

19.title: The Word of God on the white horse
19.body: Christ appears on a white horse, his vesture “dipped in blood,” and “his name is called The Word of God”; on his thigh is written “King of Kings, and Lord of Lords.”

19.question: What name is written on the rider's vesture and thigh (v. 16)?
options: King of Kings, and Lord of Lords

19.question: What happened to the beast and the false prophet (v. 20)?
options: They were cast alive into a lake of fire
They were bound for a thousand years
They were sealed in the pit

20.title: Satan bound a thousand years
20.body: An angel binds the dragon—“the Devil, and Satan”—with a great chain for a thousand years, the period known as the Millennium.

20.question: What two nations does Satan gather to battle after being loosed (v. 8)?

20.title: Gog and Magog
20.body: After the thousand years, Satan is loosed to deceive the nations “Gog and Magog” and gather them to a final battle—where fire from heaven devours them.

20.template: the dead were judged out of those things which were written in the books, according to their {0}

20.question: What is the “lake of fire” called in verse 14?

21.title: The New Jerusalem
21.body: John sees “the holy city, new Jerusalem,” coming down from God “prepared as a bride adorned for her husband”—the dwelling of God with His people.

21.template: I saw a new heaven and a new {0}

21.question: What does God promise “to him that overcometh” (v. 7)?
options: He shall inherit all things, and be God's son

21.question: Whose names are written on the twelve foundations of the city's wall (v. 14)?
options: The twelve apostles of the Lamb

21.title: A city foursquare
21.body: The holy city is “foursquare,” its length, breadth, and height equal—twelve thousand furlongs each—forming a vast cube, recalling the Holy of Holies.

21.question: What was the shape of the holy city (v. 16)?
options: Foursquare, with length, breadth, and height equal

21.title: The pearly gates
21.body: Each of the city's twelve gates is a single pearl—the origin of the phrase “the pearly gates”—and its street is “pure gold, as it were transparent glass.”

21.question: What alone may enter the holy city (v. 27)?
options: Those written in the Lamb's book of life

22.title: The tree of life
22.body: By the river of life stands the tree of life, bearing twelve kinds of fruit every month, “and the leaves of the tree were for the healing of the nations.”

22.template: was there the tree of {0}, which bare twelve manner of fruits

22.question: When John fell to worship the angel, what did the angel tell him (v. 9)?

22.title: Alpha and Omega
22.body: Near the end Jesus declares, “I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end, the first and the last”—affirming his eternal nature as the book closes.

22.question: By what title does Jesus describe himself in verse 16?
options: The root and offspring of David, the bright and morning star

22.title: Add nothing, take nothing
22.body: Revelation ends with a solemn warning: whoever adds to the words of the book will receive its plagues, and whoever takes away will lose his part in the tree of life.
